bibliographic_data.title: Half-infinite sampling and its FT
bibliographic_data.abstract: In the digital world, signals are discrete and finite. The Fourier representation of discrete and finite signals is derived from half-infinite sampling, which is a kind of sampling between finite and infinite sampling. The FTs of several half-infinite samplings are studied, showing that they are more concise than those of infinite sampling. A numerical experiment with step sampling verified the theoretical derivations.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper introduces a new kind of sampling called half-infinite sampling and derives its Fourier Transform, which is more concise than that of infinite sampling.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors derive the Fourier Transforms (FTs) of several half-infinite samplings and compare them with those of infinite sampling.
outcomes: The FT of half-infinite sampling is shown to be more concise than that of infinite sampling.
Numerical experiments, including step sampling, verified the theoretical derivations.
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, the authors introduce a new kind of sampling called half-infinite sampling. They derive the Fourier Transforms (FTs) of several half-infinite samplings and show that they are more concise than those of infinite sampling. A numerical experiment with step sampling verified these theoretical derivations. The study provides a new way to deal with finite samples, potentially solving problems such as spectrum leakage effect, Gibbs phenomenon, and end effect.
